name: Seed Blessing
description: The town blesses the season's first seeds and shares planting tips on the village common.

minigame.name: Seed Scramble
minigame.description: Blessed seed pods are tucked across the common — snatch the glowing one before it settles.

relationship.line: You came to the blessing! Here — press your thumb to the seed bowl with me. The bluff will remember it.

name: Glowtide Night
description: A lantern-lit night market along the shore as the tide glows. Stalls stay open past midnight.

minigame.name: Lantern Launch
minigame.description: Light each tide-lantern the moment it drifts past, before the current pulls it under.

relationship.line: Quiet a second — watch the tide light up. I save this spot every Glowtide. Glad it's you standing here.

name: Harvest Fair
description: A harvest market with stalls, a cook-off, and shared meals down the market lane.

minigame.name: Harvest Cook-Off
minigame.description: Plate each harvest dish the instant it's ready — let one sit and the judges move on.

relationship.line: Saved you a plate from the cook-off — still warm. Sit, eat. The fair's better with company.

name: Frostlight Festival
description: Warm cider and lanterns against the longest nights of the year, strung the length of the market lane.

minigame.name: Frostlight Lanterns
minigame.description: Kindle each frost-lantern the moment it glows, before the cold snuffs it out.

relationship.line: Cider's hot and the lanterns are lit. Stand by the brazier with me a while — winters are long, but not tonight.

yearTwo.description: The second Frostlight: more lanterns than last year, and the whole lane turns out for the cider.
yearTwo.minigameDescription: Twice the lanterns this year — kindle each glowing one before the frost takes it.
yearTwo.relationshipLine: Second Frostlight together. Same brazier, warmer crowd. Glad the town's finding its feet again.

name: Marsh Chorus
description: Reed flutes and frog-song carry across Belltide Marsh as the town gathers to sing in the thaw.

minigame.name: Marsh Catch
minigame.description: Hook each marsh-fish the instant it surfaces among the reeds.

relationship.line: You found the chorus! Take a reed flute — the marsh keeps better time than I do. Play the low note with me.

yearTwo.description: The marsh runs fuller this spring, and the chorus has new voices — the town is putting down roots.

name: Lantern Tide
description: Hundreds of paper lanterns ride the autumn tide out past the harbor mouth, each carrying a wish for the coming winter.

minigame.name: Tide Lanterns
minigame.description: Set each lantern adrift on the falling tide before the next wave breaks.

relationship.line: Write a wish on the last lantern with me — I always send one for a calm winter on the water. This year I'll send two.

yearTwo.description: The tide carries more lanterns out than ever — a whole harbor's worth of wishes for the rebuilt town.
yearTwo.relationshipLine: Two winters of wishes now. The water's been kinder since you came. Send the last one far.

name: Founders Harvest Fair
description: The town's oldest celebration, revived at last: a feast in the restored harbor honoring the founders and a year of rebuilding.

minigame.name: Founders Feast
minigame.description: Plate each founders' feast dish the instant the harbor bell rings.

relationship.line: We did it — the beacon's lit, the lane's covered, the boardwalk holds. The founders would know the place. Thank you for all of it.

yearTwo.description: The second Founders Fair fills the harbor end to end — the town it honors is whole again, and growing.
yearTwo.minigameDescription: The feast has doubled — plate every dish before the bell, the town's counting on it.
yearTwo.relationshipLine: Two years on and the harbor's never been busier. You didn't just fix the town — you brought the Fair back. That's the founders' work.
